scenarios.name: Monorepo with 3 dependent services
scenarios.desc: Merge 3 separate project repos into monorepo. Service B depends on Service A's types. Service C depends on A+B's outputs. Tests multi-repo coordination, contract validation.
scenarios.expectedValue: Prevent deployment in wrong order; catch missing type exports

scenarios.name: Microservice deployment pipeline
scenarios.desc: 5 services with startup dependencies (Cache → DB → API → Queue → Frontend). Each has build, test, deploy stages. Tests DAG ordering, gate conditions.
scenarios.expectedValue: Prevent accidentally deploying services out of order; verify all gates satisfied before deploy

scenarios.name: Upgrading core library across ecosystem
scenarios.desc: Core lib v1→v2 (breaking). 8 dependent projects must upgrade in sequence. Tests versioning, backward-compat gates, branch/merge for parallel tracks.
scenarios.expectedValue: Ensure no dependent is using v2 features before v2 released; prevent premature v1 removal

scenarios.name: CI/CD pipeline as typed DAG
scenarios.desc: Build → Test → Security scan → Staging deploy → Prod deploy. Each stage produces artifacts (binaries, reports, deployment manifests). Tests contract validation, artifact tracking.
scenarios.expectedValue: Prevent deploying before security scan runs; catch missing deployment manifests

scenarios.name: Compliance audit with checkpoints
scenarios.desc: Audit requirements in phases: discover → analyze → remediate → verify → sign-off. Each phase produces audit artifacts (reports, evidence). Tests checkpoint/restore, audit trail.
scenarios.expectedValue: Provide auditors with complete chain-of-custody for remediation; enable rollback to audit checkpoints

scenarios.name: Team onboarding as agent workflow
scenarios.desc: New engineer: env setup → codebase tour → first PR → design review → merge → deploy. Each step has agent handoff briefing. Tests orient() accuracy, agent autonomy.
scenarios.expectedValue: Reduce onboarding friction; make phase progress visible and verifiable

scenarios.name: Feature flag rollout with gates
scenarios.desc: Canary rollout: 1% → 10% → 50% → 100%. Each gate depends on previous metrics (error rate < threshold, latency ok). Tests gate conditions, progress gating.
scenarios.expectedValue: Prevent progressing to 50% if error rate is still high; provide rollback if metrics degrade

scenarios.name: Database schema evolution
scenarios.desc: Migrate schema from v1→v2→v3 (6 phases). Each phase is backward-compatible until all services upgraded. Tests versioning, backward-compat gates, verify().
scenarios.expectedValue: Prevent schema change that breaks old code; ensure all services coordinated

scenarios.name: Performance optimization sprint
scenarios.desc: Identify bottlenecks → optimize → measure → gate by improvement. Parallel optimization tracks. Tests branch/merge, gate conditions on metrics.
scenarios.expectedValue: Prevent merging optimization that doesn't meet improvement threshold; track parallel work

scenarios.name: Refactoring with dependency tracking
scenarios.desc: Refactor module A → B (depends on A) → C (depends on A+B). Parallel refactor tracks. Tests branch/merge, contract validation.
scenarios.expectedValue: Safely parallelize refactoring; prevent merge conflicts from breaking contracts

questions: How long to define your first roadmap.ts? (minutes)
Did TypeScript catch your errors upfront? (Y/N)
Did roadmap documentation feel sufficient? (1-5)
What's one thing that was confusing?
Did the DAG catch a real error in your plan? (Y/N, describe)
Would you have caught this error without this tool?
Did contract validation prevent a deployment issue? (Y/N)
How much time did this save? (estimate hours)
Could agent understand 'what to do next' from brief? (1-5)
Did orient() correctly report your position? (Y/N)
How clear was the 'produces' artifact list? (1-5)
What context was missing from the briefing?
Did merge()/branch() solve a real problem? (Y/N)
How intuitive was the merge API? (1-5)
Did checkpoint/restore match your audit needs? (Y/N)
Would you use this for multi-team coordination? (Y/N)
Would you use this in production? (Y/N)
Would you recommend to another team? (Y/N)
What's the top 3 things that would make it better?
What use case would you use it for next?
